message:
runtime/state: drop stale underscore prefixes in cancel_request tracing spans / events

Same class of drift as the epoch_gc fix: the parameters of
`RuntimeState::cancel_request` are referenced inside a
`debug_span!` and a neighbouring `info!`/`debug!` event using
underscore-prefixed names (`_source_task`, `_cleanup_budget`) that
don't match the identifiers rustc sees in scope. The function
parameter / local binding names are written without the prefix, so
the macros fail to resolve those identifiers under any build that
actually evaluates the span args.

Fix in crates/asupersync/src/runtime/state.rs:
* Rename the parameter to `source_task` (no leading underscore),
  matching the convention everywhere else in this module.
* Rename the local `let _cleanup_budget = reason.cleanup_budget();`
  to `let cleanup_budget = ...`.
* Update both the `debug_span!("cancel_request", ..., cleanup_poll_quota
  = _cleanup_budget.poll_quota, cleanup_priority = _cleanup_budget.priority,
  source_task = ?_source_task)` and the following cancellation-
  initiated event (`info!` / `debug!`) to reference
  `cleanup_budget.poll_quota`, `cleanup_budget.priority`,
  and `source_task` without underscores.

The function behavior is unchanged: `source_task` was already unused
outside of the tracing span, and `cleanup_budget` is still not
consumed anywhere except the span/event attributes. The rename
simply makes the macro inputs resolve to real identifiers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
